name: CVM-2A "Boreas"
description: The Boreas is a mid-tier cryovolatile miner employing focused 915 MHz microwave radiation to sublimate near-surface volatiles in bulk. A phased-array antenna panel directs energy into a 2 m² working patch, rapidly heating ice to sublimation temperature; the resulting gas plume is captured by a low-pressure suction shroud and piped to a condensation tank. The microwave approach avoids mechanical contact with the ice surface entirely, allowing higher throughput with less wear than thermal probes. Effective on water ice, CO2 ice, and nitrogen-dominated permafrost.
